---
title: My dynamic report
output: html_fragment
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```

```{css}
body {
  background-image: url(/Users/mustafaazami/Documents/STATS 220/Project 2/1533708768phpvTZAqF_g.jpg);
  color: white;
}

p {
  background-color: white;
  color: black;
  padding: 10px;
}

a {
  color: black; 
  text-decoration: underline
}

h2 {
  background-color: white;
  color: black;
  padding: 10px; 
}

pre {
  background-color: red;
  color: white;
  padding: 10px; 
}

```

## Survey Insights on How Students Study

This dynamic report is based on responses collected from a survey carried out on Google Form. It encompasses the preferences of students studying on the University of Auckland City Campus, such as the number of devices students would use at a time to study, where they would study on campus, and when do they study on campus. This data was formed through observations that were collected over a period of time.

```{r}
# load required packages
library(tidyverse)

url <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vRe5Q5xJYfKsVdOu0h_N2nCN3iHkk2TgeYHQifjJgZV33amJ0K7aVohF2mCQRyilsqt37AOEkvxYnxu/pub?output=csv"
logged_data <- read_csv(url)

latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(study_method = 2,
         device_number = 3,
         study_location = 4,
         study_time = 5)

# summary values
device_number_max <- max(latest_data$device_number, na.rm = TRUE)
most_frequent_location <- names(sort(table(latest_data$study_location), decreasing = TRUE))[1]

# Bar Chart 1: How many devices do students use at the same time to study?
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = device_number)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "pink") +
  labs(title = "How many devices do students use at the same time to study?",
       subtitle = "Number of devices used to study",
       x = "Number of Devices",
       y = "Responses")

# dynamic report summary comments
paste("The highest number of devices used to study at once was", device_number_max, "devices.")

# Bar Chart 2: Where do students study?
ggplot(latest_data, aes(x = study_location)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue") +
  labs(title = "Where do students study?",
       subtitle = "Locations where students tend to study on campus",
       x = "Locations", 
       y = "Responses") 

# dynamic report summary comments
paste("The most popular study location was the", most_frequent_location, "on the University of Auckland City Campus")

```
